channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Quota Bill Likely to Advantage Southern States Despite Lower Population
item.description: According to sources familiar with the matter, the proposed quota bill in India is expected to provide certain advantages to southern states, even though these states have relatively smaller populatio...

item.title: How Kenyans’ votes can shape access to better public health services
item.description: The article argues that voting is not just a political exercise but a practical tool citizens can use to demand better healthcare in Kenya. It explains that the choices Kenyans make at the ballot box ...

item.title: Study Revises Historical Causes of Hawaiian Waterbird Declines
item.description: A recent study from the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa challenges a longstanding belief that Indigenous Hawaiians drove native waterbirds to extinction. Published in Ecosphere, the research found no s...

item.title: Carney government to release spring economic update on April 28
item.description: The Carney government is set to table its federal spring economic update on April 28, 2026, according to Finance Minister Champagne. This update comes as Prime Minister Carney enters the majority era,...

item.title: Akpabio Highlights Past Fuel Scarcity, Praises Tinubu's Reforms
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio don talk say things dey better now for Nigeria with fuel, compared to how things used to be. He say back then, people were ready to spend as much as N10,000 per litre...

item.title: Government proposes raising Lok Sabha seats to 850 to implement women’s reservation by 2029
item.description: The BJP-led central government has proposed a constitutional amendment to increase the number of Lok Sabha seats from 543 to 850, aiming to implement 33% reservation for women by the 2029 general elec...

item.title: Regular mental health check-ins provide a clearer picture of student wellbeing
item.description: Students' mental health is a growing concern in schools, with half of adult mental health issues beginning before the age of 14. Despite this, many children experiencing challenges do not receive prof...

item.title: IMF revises India’s GDP growth forecast to 6.5% for FY 2026-27
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has updated its economic outlook for India, projecting a 6.5% growth rate for the financial year 2026-27, slightly higher than its earlier forecast of 6.4% made i...

item.title: ABS-CBN Introduces 'Mugna' as Word of the Day for April 15, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN, one of the Philippines' leading media and entertainment companies, presents the Word of the Day for April 15, 2026: 'mugna'. This daily feature aims to enrich Filipino vocabulary and promote ...

item.title: ADC Leadership Dispute: Court Hears Arguments Amidst INEC Actions and Potential Legal Repercussions
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja is currently embroiled in a complex legal battle concerning the leadership of the African Democratic Congress (ADC). Gombe, a former deputy national chairman, initiated t...

item.title: Gujarat Election Officer Dies by Suicide Ahead of Local Polls
item.description: A deeply concerning incident has unfolded in Valsad, Gujarat, where an election officer has tragically died by suicide just weeks before scheduled local body polls. The news has sent shockwaves throug...

item.title: U.S. Senate Panel Signals Likely Reduction in Pentagon’s Iran War Funding Ask
item.description: U.S. lawmakers are signaling that the Pentagon is expected to scale back its request for additional funding to finance the ongoing war against Iran, after initial plans called for more than 200 billio...

item.title: Government announces major increase in fuel prices following global cost adjustments
item.description: Kenyans are set to bear the brunt of a sharp increase in fuel costs after the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) announced new pump prices that have gone up by as much as Sh40 per litre....

item.title: Bisdak Singers Talk About Their Most Memorable Rainbow Rumble Experiences
item.description: In a recent feature, popular Bisdak singers Jason, Marielle, Sofronio, Mark, and Dulce shared their most cherished moments from the reality singing competition “Rainbow Rumble.” Each artist reflected ...

item.title: Researchers Directly Measure Key Proton Capture in Formation of Rare Cosmic Elements
item.description: A team of international researchers led by Artemis Tsantiri has achieved a major breakthrough in understanding the formation of rare proton-rich isotopes, known as p-nuclei, in the universe. For the f...

item.title: Widow of Former OPP Officer Alleges Misconduct by Saint John Police Chief in Court Filings
item.description: Heather Harmer, the widow of former Ontario Provincial Police officer Jeffrey Harmer, has filed lawsuits against the OPP and its police union alleging misconduct by Saint John Police Chief Robert Bruc...

item.title: DSWD ensures continued support for vulnerable households through Walang Gutom Program amid rising food prices
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) reassured the public that beneficiaries of the Walang Gutom Program (WGP) will continue receiving free meals despite the ongoing rise in food co...

item.title: Woman removed from Manchester nightclub despite legal rights as a wheelchair user
item.description: Maddie Haining, an 18-year-old from Oldham, recounted her experience of being asked to leave Club Tropicana in Manchester's Gay Village due to her wheelchair, which staff claimed posed a safety risk. ...

item.title: Comelec reports nearly 4 million voter registrations for upcoming barangay and SK polls
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has confirmed that a total of 3.91 million voter registration applications have been processed for the upcoming barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) elections. ...

item.title: Enforcing Child Support for Children When a Parent Lives Abroad
item.description: In Kenya, children enjoy strong legal protection under the Constitution and the Children’s Act, which ensure parental responsibility is not dependent on marital status. Both parents, whether married o...
